violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the side of the store, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025

Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Dishes and utensils were removed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils ice machine were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was emptied and put out of service at the time of inspection. Ice machine is not to be used until it is cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Soup, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Per PIC, soup was made yesterday and was observed at 46 DF. Soup was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Proper cooling methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Beans, chorizo, and chicken wings was observed held between 95-110 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Chicken, rice, and chorizo was observed held on the prep table between 55-60 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Beef was observed thawing in the prep sink at ambient temperature. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Beef was moved to walk-in cooler at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front and side of the store, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
The 3-bay sink pipe was observed leaking into a container. Establishment is to fix leak. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a container. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/14/2023

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. Knives were observed stored between a sink and the wall. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/14/2023

Single service items are not being stored properly. Single Service items were observed stored on the floor. Single Service items must be stored at least 6 inches off the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the kitchen have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023
The tile floor in the bakery area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023
